entries.value: A radio button may not be used on its own: it must be rendered in a group of related radio buttons using a radio group.
It should be visible at a glance if a radio button has been selected, and selected items should be more visually prominent than unselected items.
Radio buttons can be selected or unselected. Radio buttons have enabled, disabled, hover, focused and pressed states.
Radio buttons are used for mutually exclusive choices, not for multiple choices.
button can be selected at a time. When a user chooses a new item, the previous choice is automatically deselected.
Can be used in tiles, data tables, modals, side panels, and in forms on full pages.
We recommend radio button labels being fewer than three words.
If you are tight on space, consider rewording the label. Do not truncate radio button label text with an ellipsis.
A radio input allows users to select only one option from a number of choices. Radio is generally displayed in a radio group.
Radio buttons are best used for selecting a single option from a list of mutually exclusive options.
Radio buttons let people select one option from a set of option
A radio input allows users to select only one option from a number of choices. Radio is generally displayed in a radio group
Use radio buttons when you have a group of mutually exclusive choices and only one selection from the group is allowed.
Radio button is a form control for making a single selection from a short list of options.
A radio lets users select a single item from a mutually exclusive list.
Use radio buttons when only one item can be selected from a list.
Do use checkboxes when multiple items can be selected.
Radio button labels are positioned to the right of their inputs in languages that read left to right.
If there is a radio button grouping, they can be laid out vertically or horizontally depending on the use case and the structure of the UI.
Radio buttons in a form should be placed at least 32px (layout-03) below or before the next component.
Spacing of 24px (layout-02) or 16px (layout-01) can also be used when space is more restricted or if the form is more complex.
Do let text wrap beneath the radio button so the control and label are top aligned.
Use radio buttons for mutually exclusive options
Don't use checkboxes when only one item can be selected from a list. Use radio buttons instead.
